verses.text: Be not envious of evil men nor desire their company, for their hearts study destruction; 'through wisdom is an house builded,' and by knowledge its rooms filled with riches. Envy not the wicked. Wisdom, not mischief, builds a lasting house.

Be not envious of evil men nor desire their company, for their hearts study destruction; 'through wisdom is an house builded,' and by knowledge its rooms filled with riches. Envy not the wicked. Wisdom, not mischief, builds a lasting house.

Be not envious of evil men nor desire their company, for their hearts study destruction; 'through wisdom is an house builded,' and by knowledge its rooms filled with riches. Envy not the wicked. Wisdom, not mischief, builds a lasting house.

'A wise man is strong'; in multitude of counsellors there is safety in war; wisdom is too high for a fool, who is silent in the gate. Wisdom is might. Counsel wins battles, but the foolish have nothing to say where it matters.

'A wise man is strong'; in multitude of counsellors there is safety in war; wisdom is too high for a fool, who is silent in the gate. Wisdom is might. Counsel wins battles, but the foolish have nothing to say where it matters.

'A wise man is strong'; in multitude of counsellors there is safety in war; wisdom is too high for a fool, who is silent in the gate. Wisdom is might. Counsel wins battles, but the foolish have nothing to say where it matters.

He that devises evil is called a mischievous person; 'the thought of foolishness is sin'; 'if thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength is small.' Even plotting is sin. Trouble tests and reveals the true measure of our strength.

He that devises evil is called a mischievous person; 'the thought of foolishness is sin'; 'if thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength is small.' Even plotting is sin. Trouble tests and reveals the true measure of our strength.

Deliver those drawn toward death, and hold not back; do not plead ignorance, for He that ponders the heart knows, and 'shall not he render to every man according to his works?' Rescue the perishing. God sees our excuses and repays each deed.

Deliver those drawn toward death, and hold not back; do not plead ignorance, for He that ponders the heart knows, and 'shall not he render to every man according to his works?' Rescue the perishing. God sees our excuses and repays each deed.

Eat honey, for it is good; so is wisdom to the soul, with a sure reward; lay not wait against the just, for 'a just man falleth seven times, and riseth up again,' but the wicked fall into mischief. Wisdom is sweet and sure. The righteous, though they stumble, rise again.

'Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth,' lest the LORD see it and turn His wrath away from him. Gloating displeases God. We are not to delight in another's downfall, even a foe's.
'Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth,' lest the LORD see it and turn His wrath away from him. Gloating displeases God. We are not to delight in another's downfall, even a foe's.

Fret not over evildoers nor envy the wicked, 'for there shall be no reward to the evil man'; fear the LORD and the king, and meddle not with the rebellious, for their ruin rises suddenly. The wicked have no future. Reverence God and shun rebellion.

It is not good to respect persons in judgment; he who calls the wicked righteous shall be cursed, but those who rebuke him shall have delight and blessing. Justice must be impartial. To bless what God condemns earns a curse.

A right answer is like a kiss on the lips; prepare your work outside, then build your house; be no witness against your neighbour without cause, nor repay evil for evil. Order your labour wisely. Speak truth, and leave revenge to God.

Passing the sluggard's field, I saw it overgrown with thorns and its wall broken; 'a little sleep, a little slumber... so shall thy poverty come.' Sloth ruins by degrees. Small indulgences of laziness end in sudden want.
